_comment: English → Japanese | Month 1 Week 1 | Pronunciation — Hiragana Vowels
title: Pronunciation: Japanese Vowels & Hiragana Sounds
description: Master the 5 core Japanese vowel sounds and the rules of Japanese pronunciation.

blocks.title: Japanese Pronunciation Rules
points: Japanese vowels NEVER change — A is always 'ah', I is always 'ee', etc.
No stress accent in Japanese — all syllables are pronounced with equal weight
Long vowels: hold the vowel sound twice as long — おかあさん (okaa-san)
The 'u' sound in す (su) and つ (tsu) is often very soft/devoiced
Japanese pronunciation is VERY consistent — no silent letters!

blocks.title: Sound Differences — Listen Carefully
blocks.instruction: What's the difference between these pairs?

pairs.diff: Mouth openness — あ is wider, お is slightly rounded
Tongue height — い is high, え is mid
Voiced vs unvoiced: the dakuten (゛) mark voices the consonant

words.note: All syllables equal weight — no stress in Japanese!
9 syllables — practice slowly, then speed up

blocks.title: Japanese Tongue Twister (言葉遊び)
blocks.text: なまむぎ なまごめ なまたまご
blocks.translation: Raw wheat, raw rice, raw egg
blocks.tip: Focus on the 'na-ma' sound — repeat slowly: na-ma-mu-gi, na-ma-go-me, na-ma-ta-ma-go!

blocks.label: Hiragana vowel pronunciation
blocks.transcript: あ (a) — い (i) — う (u) — え (e) — お (o)
